nodes.id: loc-諾爾埃普夫墨爾加多國家公園-69614d18
nodes.name: 諾爾埃普夫墨爾加多國家公園
nodes.summary: 諾爾埃普夫墨爾加多國家公園是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括紫藍金剛鸚鵡，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 潘塔納爾濕地
nodes.summary: 潘塔納爾濕地是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联22种鸟类，包括Bare-faced curassow、Maguari stork、黑冠白颈鹭等22种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Caatinga
nodes.summary: Caatinga是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联56种鸟类，包括白腹拟䳍、黄腿穴䳍、White-browed guan等56种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Mongabay
nodes.summary: Mongabay是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括紫藍金剛鸚鵡，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 湿地
nodes.summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

nodes.name: 约翰·莱瑟姆 (鸟类学家)
nodes.summary: 约翰·莱瑟姆 (鸟类学家)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联50种鸟类，包括红耳鸭、斑头雁、鬃林鸭等50种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联694种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等694种鸟类。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 紫藍金剛鸚鵡
nodes.summary: 紫藍金剛鸚鵡（Hyacinth macaw，学名 Anodorhynchus hyacinthinus），在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，已关联6个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括沼泽、稀树草原、湿地，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏。
locations: 諾爾埃普夫墨爾加多國家公園
San Matías Integrated Management Natural Area

nodes.name: 稀树草原
nodes.summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

nodes.name: San Matías Integrated Management Natural Area
nodes.summary: San Matías Integrated Management Natural Area是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括紫藍金剛鸚鵡，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.key: bird-紫藍金剛鸚鵡-405ab1fe__distributed_in__loc-约翰-莱瑟姆-鸟类学家-1edb5695
links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: --> | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | status2 = CITES_A1 | status2_system = CITES | status2_ref = | genus = Anodorhynchus | species = hyacinthinus | authority = (Latham, 1790) | range_map = Anodorhynchus hyacinthinus map.svg }} The hyacinth macaw (Anodorhynchus hyacinthinus), or hyacinthine macaw, is a parrot native to central and eastern South America

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |last=Franco Berton |first=Eduardo |date=16 May 2018 |website=Mongabay Latam |language=es |publisher=Environmental News |access-date=31 October 2021 }} and locals often feed the macaws maize, like chickens. In the early 1990s it became apparent the species also occurred in the remote Noel Kempff Mercado National Park area a few hundred kilometres northward. Most of the Bolivian population of this macaw is thought to be found in the San Matías Integrated Management Natural Area, an area with extensive pantanal (swamp).{{cite journal |last1=Pinto-Ledezma |first1=Jesús N

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: In different areas of their range, these parrots are found in savannah grasslands, in dry thorn forests known as caatinga, and in palm stands or swamps, particularly the moriche palm (Mauritia flexuosa).{{Cite book |first=Joseph M

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: |last=Forshaw |author-link=Joseph Forshaw |title=Parrots of the World; an Identification Guide |others=Illustrated by Frank Knight |page=95 |publisher=Princeton University Press |isbn=978-0-691-09251-5 |year=2006 |url-access=registration |url=https://archive.org/details/parrotsofworldid0000fors }} A 2014 Bolivian study in San Matías Integrated Management Natural Area which correlated sightings to habitat found that areas including seasonally inundated savannas, wetlands and anthropogenic areas habitats interspersed with a mosaic of savannas, were the best indicators for the presence of the macaws

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Encyclopedia of Life. A few serious threats to the survival of the species in the Pantanal include human activities, mainly those resulting in habitat loss, the burning of land for pasture maintenance, and illegal trapping{{cite journal |author=Pinho, J
